title: Numerical Linear Algebra and Optimization · Model Repair Bay
summary: Build, test, and communicate a model involving large constrained computational models. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a reproducible numerical linear optimization computational study and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Formulate direct iterative solvers in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive matrix conditioning in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute gradient methods in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-advanced cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Recalculate the repaired case
steps.instruction: A model passes through (1, 5) and (5, 17). Calculate its average rate of change. Use the result to decide Automatic Differentiation for a reproducible numerical linear optimization computational study.
steps.why: The Automatic Differentiation result for a reproducible numerical linear optimization computational study is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (17 − 5) / (5 − 1). Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Communicate automatic differentiation in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
